content.text:
<timestamp>Wednesday, Apr 29, 2026, 11:22 AM (UTC-4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
what would be the best external authentication solution you would recommend for this codebase?
</user_query>
I need to explore the codebase first to understand the current authentication setup before recommending an external solution.

I've gathered the key context. Before recommending a solution, I need to clarify some scope. Here's what I found in the codebase:

- There is already a dedicated auth app at [apps/auth](apps/auth) (TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers) that wraps **AWS Cognito** with a custom UI — see [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md).
- `apps/org-next` is a downstream client only. It reads `httpOnly` session cookies (`authToken`, `refreshToken`, `__session`) set by the auth app — see [apps/org-next/docs/README.md](apps/org-next/docs/README.md) (lines 26–56) and [apps/org-next/src/types/auth.ts](apps/org-next/src/types/auth.ts).
- The flow is OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE; org-next only `window.location.assign(env.VITE_AUTH_APP_URL)` and validates 401s through middleware — see `src/routes/-auth.ts`, `src/server/middleware.ts`, `src/server/session.server.ts`.
- The app is multi-tenant (tenants/organizations bootstrap in `_authed`), B2B-shaped, and the backend (`anedot_next`) appears to validate Cognito-issued JWTs.

So "external auth solution" could mean very different things here, and the right recommendation depends heavily on which you mean.

 I'm seeing that the codebase already has a dedicated auth app using AWS Cognito with OAuth2/OIDC and PKCE, routed through a custom AnedotAuth service on Cloudflare Workers.

The user's question about external auth solutions is pretty open-ended, so I need to clarify whether they're looking to replace Cognito entirely or optimize their current setup. Given the signals in the codebase—B2B SaaS with multi-tenancy, TanStack Start, and an existing Cognito investment—I should ask what constraints matter most to them: cost, developer experience, B2B-specific features, or multi-tenancy support.

questions.prompt: What's the scope of the recommendation you want?

options.label: Replace AWS Cognito as the underlying identity provider (e.g. Auth0, Clerk, WorkOS, Supabase, Better Auth)
Keep Cognito but replace the custom apps/auth shell with a hosted/managed UI
Just for apps/org-next as a client (e.g. better OIDC client, session helpers, no change to apps/auth or backend)
Pretend it's greenfield — what would you recommend if starting fresh today?

questions.prompt: What's driving the question? (pick the most important)

options.label: Developer experience / faster iteration on auth UI and flows
B2B features (SSO/SAML, SCIM, multi-tenant orgs, RBAC) for enterprise customers
Compliance, security posture, or audit requirements
Specific missing features (passkeys, magic links, social, MFA UX, etc.)
Just exploring options, no specific pain point

content.text:
Got it. Let me also peek at backend constraints and the OpenAPI to make sure my recommendation lines up with what the API expects (token format, etc.).

 I'm thinking through what would work best for their greenfield TanStack Start project — a multi-tenant B2B SaaS with a Rails-like backend — considering their priorities around developer experience, cost, and security.

Looking at the main contenders: Clerk offers the smoothest developer experience but gets expensive at scale; WorkOS AuthKit is free up to 1M monthly active users with strong B2B features and decent DX, making it increasingly popular for this use case; Better Auth gives great DX and zero vendor costs but puts the security burden on their team since they'd self-host it.

For a B2B SaaS at their scale, the tradeoff seems to be between WorkOS's generous free tier and built-in B2B capabilities versus Better Auth's cost savings if they're comfortable operating their own auth infrastructure. WorkOS AuthKit stands out as the practical choice here—it's free at the scale most startups operate at, handles B2B concerns natively with organizations and SSO built in, and the security credentials (SOC2, ISO 27001) mean I don't have to worry about compliance later. I'll lead with that recommendation and mention Better Auth and Clerk as solid alternatives depending on their specific constraints.

I have enough to make a grounded recommendation. Let me write it up as a plan.

 Looking at the full picture now, I can see that since the backend is flexible with OIDC specs in a greenfield scenario, I'm not locked into Cognito and can evaluate options based on developer experience, cost, and security. TanStack Start with Cloudflare Workers seems to be emerging as a strong candidate given the constraints around these factors.

WorkOS AuthKit stands out as the strongest overall choice for a B2B SaaS platform—it's free up to 1M monthly active users, has built-in org and SSO capabilities, and maintains the same OIDC pattern already in use. Clerk offers the smoothest developer experience with React components and hooks, but pricing escalates quickly with org and SSO features. Better Auth provides the most cost-effective option as an open-source solution that runs in your own database, giving you full control and TypeScript-native tooling, though you'd be responsible for managing the security operations yourself.

I'm thinking through how to present this as a structured recommendation—the user asked for guidance on which to choose, so I should lay out the decision clearly with the tradeoffs, then offer to help with implementation details if they want to move forward.

input.name: auth recommendation
input.overview: Greenfield external auth recommendation for this TanStack Start + multi-tenant B2B SaaS workspace, optimizing for DX, cost, and security simultaneously.
input.plan:
# Recommended External Auth: WorkOS AuthKit ## TL;DR For a greenfield rebuild of this codebase, I recommend **WorkOS AuthKit** as the primary auth solution, with **Better Auth** as the strong open-source alternative if you want to avoid any vendor and **Clerk** as the alternative if pure React DX outranks cost. Cognito (the current pick under [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md)) would not be my first choice greenfield given the DX trade-offs you already felt — it's the reason you have a whole [apps/auth](apps/auth) shell wrapping it. ## Why WorkOS AuthKit fits this workspace This is a B2B, multi-tenant SaaS: - `_authed` in [apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx) bootstraps a tenant and redirects to `/organizations/new` if none exists. - The architecture under [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md) is already a pure OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE redirect, with the backend at [anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml](anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml) validating bearer JWTs. WorkOS hits all three of your priorities: - **DX** - Drop-in hosted UI (AuthKit) replaces almost all of [apps/auth](apps/auth) — the spec'd Sign In, Sign Up, Confirm, Forgot, Reset, Force Change Password, MFA Challenge screens (~8 screens in [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md)) are all built and maintained by WorkOS. - First-class **Organizations** primitive maps directly to your `tenants` model, so the bootstrap dance in [apps/org-next/docs/README.md](apps/org-next/docs/README.md) (lines 58–63) becomes simpler — `org_id` is in the JWT. - Standard OIDC, so the existing pattern in [apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts) (httpOnly access/refresh cookies + middleware that catches 401s) ports over almost unchanged. - **Cost** - **1,000,000 MAU free** on the core tier (auth, orgs, social login, MFA, magic links, passkeys). Almost no realistic SaaS hits this in years 1–2. - You only pay per-connection for premium B2B features (SAML SSO, SCIM directory sync) — and those are revenue-justified because enterprise customers ask for them. - Compared to Clerk (~$0.02/MAU after 10k + per-org/SSO adders) you save a lot at scale. - **Security** - SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR — vendor handles JWKS rotation, password hashing, breach detection, bot defense, brute-force protection. - Standard OIDC JWT means the backend ([anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml](anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml)) keeps validating bearer tokens via JWKS — only the issuer changes, no custom crypto on your side. - Built-in MFA (TOTP, SMS, passkeys) without you owning the UX from [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md) sections 6–7. ## Why not the others - **AWS Cognito (current)** - Cheap, but the DX is exactly why you built [apps/auth](apps/auth) — Hosted UI is rigid, custom-UI requires you to call `initiateAuth` / `respondToAuthChallenge` server-side ([apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md) sections 1, 6, 7) and own every screen, every challenge state, every error mapping. - No first-class organizations/SSO/SCIM — you'd have to build tenant→user mapping yourself. - Aggressive throttling and surprising error semantics (e.g. `UserNotFoundException` parity). - **Clerk** — best React DX overall, but cost ramps quickly past 10k MAU (~$0.02/MAU plus per-org and per-SSO-connection charges), and it's Next-first; TanStack Start works but is less idiomatic. - **Auth0** — mature and secure, but the most expensive of the bunch at scale and DX has stagnated. Avoid unless an enterprise deal mandates it. - **Supabase / Firebase Auth** — fine for B2C, weak for B2B (no real orgs, SSO, SCIM stories). Wrong tool for this shape. - **Better Auth** — genuinely strong runner-up: free, OSS, TS-native, plugin-based with `organization`, `sso`, `mfa`, `passkey` plugins, fits TanStack Start cleanly. Pick this if you want zero vendor lock-in and are comfortable owning the security ops (JWKS rotation, hardening, breach response). For most B2B teams, WorkOS's compliance posture is worth the trade. ## How it would slot into this codebase ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph workspace [Anedot workspace] OrgNext[apps org-next
TanStack Start] OtherApps[other client apps] end AuthKit[WorkOS AuthKit
hosted UI] WorkOS[WorkOS API
orgs SSO SCIM] Backend[anedot_next API
JWT validation via JWKS] OrgNext -->|"OIDC redirect"| AuthKit AuthKit --> WorkOS WorkOS -->|"code -> tokens"| OrgNext OrgNext -->|"Bearer JWT"| Backend Backend -->|"JWKS"| WorkOS ``` What changes vs today: - **Delete or shrink [apps/auth](apps/auth)**: AuthKit hosts every screen in [apps/auth/docs/spec.md](apps/auth/docs/spec.md). Keep a thin redirect endpoint only if you want a branded entry URL. - **Keep the org-next session model**: the cookie shape in [apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts) and the 401-redirect middleware keep working — just point the OIDC provider at WorkOS instead of Cognito and read the `org_id` claim into `selectedTenantId`. - **Backend ([anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml](anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml))**: rename `CognitoJwt` security scheme to `WorkOSJwt` (still `bearer`/`JWT`); swap the JWKS URL. No business logic changes. - **Tenants**: map WorkOS Organizations 1:1 to your tenant model. Membership becomes a WorkOS API call instead of a custom API. ## Decision matrix - DX: Clerk > **WorkOS** ≈ Better Auth > Auth0 > Cognito - Cost at 100k MAU: Better Auth (free infra) > **WorkOS** (still free) > Cognito (~cheap) > Auth0 / Clerk (expensive) - Security posture (vendor-managed): Auth0 ≈ **WorkOS** ≈ Clerk > Cognito > Better Auth (you own ops) - B2B fit (orgs/SSO/SCIM): **WorkOS** > Auth0 ≈ Clerk > Better Auth > Cognito WorkOS is the only option in the top group on **all three** of your stated priorities. ## Suggested next step If you want, I can follow this with one of: - A concrete migration plan from Cognito → WorkOS AuthKit for [apps/auth](apps/auth) and [apps/org-next](apps/org-next) (file-by-file). - A side-by-side spike plan comparing WorkOS AuthKit vs Better Auth in a throwaway worktree. - A PoC plan for just dropping AuthKit into [apps/org-next](apps/org-next) without touching the backend yet.
